cards.back: A mathematical model for computation that consists of a tape divided into cells, each of which can hold a symbol from a finite alphabet
A set of well-defined instructions intended to solve a particular problem or perform a specific task
A measure of the amount of time or space needed to solve a problem by a given algorithm, often expressed in Big O notation
A type of finite automaton that can change its state in response to input symbols from an alphabet
A pattern used to match strings in a specific format, often used in text processing and parsing
